In the libnn project at /local/nick/libnn, the user wants to understand how to specify toolchain, QEMU, and FPGA info. Search for:

1. How the toolchain (compiler prefix/path) is specified — look at build.sh and test.sh
2. How QEMU is configured/used as a simulator — search for QEMU references in test.sh and any config files
3. How FPGA/BOARD targets are configured — search for FPGA, BOARD references in test scripts and any related config

Be thorough — check build.sh, test.sh, Makefile*, and any config/setup scripts. Report the relevant mechanisms and examples.

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→#Input:
     4→# $1: CPU name (case insensitive)
     5→#       this could be N10/D10/D15/N225/D23/N25/NX25/D25/AX25/A27/AX27/N45/D45/NX45/AX45/NX27V
     6→# $2: Extra compilation flags for the library
     7→#       if this parameter is set, these extra flags will
     8→#       be appended to the default compilation flags
     9→#       ; otherwise, the default compilation flags
    10→#       defined in this script will be applied.
    11→# $3: Specified compiler
    12→#       If this parameter is left to be empty, the
    13→#       nds32le-elf-gcc (for V3),
    14→#       riscv32-elf-gcc (for RV32),
    15→#       riscv64-elf-gcc (for RV64) will be used as default compiler.
    16→# $4: Library output path
    17→#       As thsi parameter is set, the built library and objects will be saved
    18→#       into the user specified path; otherwise, the built library and
    19→#       objects will be saved in the root of this repository.
    20→#
    21→# Example:
    22→#   ./build.sh D10
    23→# Or
    24→#   ./build.sh D10 "-funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=2"
    25→# Or
    26→#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc
    27→# Or
    28→#   ./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang
    29→###############################################################################
    30→
    31→#----- build switches -----
    32→BUILD_LIBNN=1
    33→BUILD_DOC=0
    34→MAKE_INSTALL=1
    35→
    36→# common compilation flags
    37→CFLAG="-Wall -Werror -O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections"
    38→
    39→#convert CPU name to lower case
    40→CPU=$(echo ${1} | tr A-Z a-z)
    41→
    42→#set the default compilation flags
    43→if [ "${CPU}" == "n10" ]; then
    44→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=n10 -std=c99"
    45→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
    46→elif [ "${CPU}" == "d10" ]; then
    47→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d10 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -std=c99"
    48→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
    49→elif [ "${CPU}" == "d15" ]; then
    50→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mcpu=d15 -mext-dsp -mext-zol -funroll-loops --param max-unroll-times=4 -fno-auto-inc-dec -std=c99"
    51→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="nds32le-elf-"
    52→elif [ "${CPU}" == "n225" ]; then
    53→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    54→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    55→elif [ "${CPU}" == "d23" ]; then
    56→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-23-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    57→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    58→elif [ "${CPU}" == "n25" ]; then
    59→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    60→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    61→elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25" ]; then
    62→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    63→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    64→elif [ "${CPU}" == "d25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a25" ]; then
    65→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    66→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    67→elif [ "${CPU}" == "dx25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "ax25" ]; then
    68→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    69→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    70→elif [ "${CPU}" == "a27" ]; then
    71→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    72→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
    73→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax27" ]; then
    74→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    75→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    76→elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25v_freya" ]; then

    78→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    79→elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx27v" ]; then

    81→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    82→elif [ "${CPU}" == "n45" ]; then
    83→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"

    85→elif [ "${CPU}" == "d45" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a45" ]; then

    88→elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx45" ]; then

    91→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45" ]; then

    93→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    94→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv" ]; then
    95→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    96→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
    97→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv_ace" ]; then
    98→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${PWD}/ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
    99→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   100→elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mp" ]; then
   101→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
   102→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
   103→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mp" ]; then
   104→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
   105→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   106→elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mpv" ]; then
   107→    if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
   108→        CC_TMP="${3}"
   109→    else
   110→        CC_TMP="riscv32-elf-gcc"
   111→    fi
   112→
   113→    ${CC_TMP} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
   114→    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
   115→    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
   116→        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
   117→    else
   118→        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
   119→    fi

   121→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
   122→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
   123→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mpv" ]; then
   124→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
   125→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   126→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax65" ]; then
   127→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
   128→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   129→elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax66" ]; then
   130→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
   131→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
   132→elif [ "${CPU}" == "x86" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "x64" ]; then
   133→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing"
   134→    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX=""
   135→else
   136→    >&2 echo "Usage: build.sh CPU_NAME EXTRA_COMPILATION_FLAGS"
   137→    >&2 echo
   138→    >&2 echo "${CPU} is not a supported CPU. Please input the supported CPU name as below."
   139→    >&2 echo "The supported CPUs are: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27, N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV or AX46MPV (case insensitive)"
   140→    exit 1
   141→fi
   142→
   143→# compiler
   144→if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
   145→    CC="${3}"
   146→else
   147→    CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
   148→fi
   149→
   150→# customized optimization for 45-series
   151→if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ mtune=andes-45-series ]]; then
   152→    CFLAG+=" -DENA_45_SERIES_OPT"
   153→fi
   154→
   155→# extra compilation flags
   156→if [ -n "${2}" ]; then
   157→    CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${2}"
   158→fi
   159→
   160→# output path
   161→LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
   162→if [[ -n ${4} ]]; then
   163→    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${4}"
   164→else
   165→    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}"
   166→fi
   167→
   168→#==================== extra optional flags ====================
   169→# -----
   170→# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
   171→# Note. We should check the zfh extension could be enabled or not with
   172→# the extra compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
   173→# toolchain; however, it's disabled by the extra compilation flags.
   174→# -----
   175→# check the zfh extension could be enabled or not
   176→${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
   177→if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   178→    # check the toolchain is [single|double]-abi or not
   179→    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
   180→    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
   181→    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
   182→        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series
   183→        if [[ ${CPU} == nx27v ]] || [[ ${CPU} == *45* ]]; then
   184→            CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mzfh"
   185→        fi
   186→    fi
   187→fi
   188→
   189→INCLUDE_CFLAG="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal -I${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include"
   190→GLOBAL_CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${INCLUDE_CFLAG}"
   191→RELEASE_FOLDER="${LIB_ROOT}/release"
   192→
   193→# generate nds_version.c to make library version number
   194→cd ${LIB_ROOT}
   195→nds_autogen_info.sh "libnn"
   196→if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
   197→    mv ${LIB_ROOT}/nds_version.c "${LIB_ROOT}/Source"
   198→fi
   199→
   200→if [ ${BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
   201→    # take 1/16 processors to build the library parallelly
   202→    nprocessors=$(getconf _NPROCESSORS_ONLN)
   203→    nprocessors=$((nprocessors / 16))
   204→    if [[ ${nprocessors} -lt 1 ]]; then
   205→        # for the case of total processor number is less than 16
   206→        nprocessors=1;
   207→    fi
   208→
   209→    if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
   210→        cd ${LIB_ROOT}
   211→
   212→        AR="${CC%-*}-ar"
   213→        AR_FLAG="crD"
   214→        libhvm_branch="ast-v5_4_0-release"
   215→        libnhvm_repo_dir="${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project"
   216→        libhvm_name="libhvm"
   217→        libhvm_build_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm.log"
   218→        libhvm_build_error_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm_error.log"
   219→
   220→        # clone the libhvm repo and switch to the proper branch
   221→        echo "**********************************************"
   222→        echo "Clone and update libhvm ..."
   223→        git submodule update --init --recursive --remote
   224→        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   225→        git reset --hard ${libhvm_branch}
   226→
   227→        # disable the HVM bank awared
   228→        sed -i 's/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t1/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t0/' ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm/src/common/nds_hvm_config.h
   229→
   230→        # build the libhvm
   231→        echo "Start building libhvm ..."
   232→        cp ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/Makefile_libhvm ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   233→        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
   234→        make clean -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*}
   235→        make -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*} EXTRA_CFLAGS="-mno-16-bit" V=1 > ${libhvm_build_log} 2> ${libhvm_build_error_log}
   236→        mv ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm.a ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}
   237→        echo "Building libhvm done ..."
   238→        echo "**********************************************"
   239→
   240→        cd ${LIB_ROOT}  # go back to the repo root
   241→    fi
   242→
   243→    echo "**********************************************"
   244→    echo " Building Andes NN Library ..."
   245→    echo "**********************************************"
   246→    echo
   247→    make clean -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
   248→    make -j${nprocessors} -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak CFLAGS="${GLOBAL_CFLAG}" CROSS_COMPILE="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}" CC="${CC}" BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"
   249→    ret=$?
   250→    echo
   251→    echo "**********************************************"
   252→    if [[ ${ret} -eq 0 ]]; then
   253→        echo "Andes NN Library build successful."
   254→    else
   255→        echo "Andes NN Library build failed!!!"
   256→    fi
   257→    echo "**********************************************"
   258→    echo
   259→fi
   260→
   261→#Document generation
   262→if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
   263→    CUR_DIR=`pwd`
   264→    cd ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs
   265→    rm -rf ./docs
   266→    echo "**********************************************"
   267→    echo " Generating document for libnn library ..."
   268→    echo "**********************************************"
   269→    echo
   270→    doxygen Doxyfile > log_doxygen.txt && cd docs/latex && make > log_make_latex.txt 2>&1
   271→    mv log_make_latex.txt ${LIB_ROOT}
   272→    cd ..
   273→    mkdir -p pdf
   274→    cp latex/refman.pdf pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf
   275→    cp rtf/refman.rtf rtf/libnn_user_manual.rtf
   276→    cd ${CUR_DIR}
   277→    echo
   278→    echo "Done!"
   279→    echo "Andes NN Library (libnn) User Manual is generated with two format:"
   280→    echo "html folder: Docs/docs/html"
   281→    echo "PDF file: Docs/docs/pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf"
   282→    echo "**********************************************"
   283→fi  #BUILD_DOC
   284→
   285→#Installation
   286→if [ ${MAKE_INSTALL} -eq 1 ] && [ ${ret} -eq 0 ]; then
   287→    mkdir -p ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   288→    rm -rf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}/*
   289→    cp -f ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/libnn.a ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   290→    cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Include ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   291→
   292→    if [ ${BUILD_DOC} -eq 1 ]; then
   293→        cp -rf ${LIB_ROOT}/Docs/docs/pdf ${RELEASE_FOLDER}
   294→    fi
   295→    echo "**********************************************"
   296→    echo " Andes NN Library installation done!"
   297→    echo " install folder: ${RELEASE_FOLDER}"
   298→    echo "**********************************************"
   299→    echo
   300→fi  #MAKE_INSTALL
   301→
   302→exit $ret

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→#
     4→# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
     5→# performance.
     6→#
     7→# Input:
     8→#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
     9→#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
    10→#       of libdsp.
    11→#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
    12→#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
    13→#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
    14→#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
    15→#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
    16→#       environment variable before using this script.
    17→#   $3: Simulator name
    18→#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
    19→#   $4: Simulation target
    20→#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
    21→#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
    22→#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
    23→#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
    24→#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
    25→#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
    26→#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
    27→#              will be verified in this mode.
    28→#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
    29→#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
    30→#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
    31→#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
    32→#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
    33→#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
    34→#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
    35→#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
    36→#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
    37→#       used to build the library.
    38→#   $7: Specified linker
    39→#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
    40→#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
    41→#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
    42→#
    43→# Example:
    44→#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
    45→#
    46→#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
    47→#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
    48→#
    49→#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
    50→#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"
    51→#
    52→#   Ex-3: use lld as linker, clang as compiler then test DSP algorithm with RV64 SID
    53→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-clang riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp" lld
    54→#
    55→#   Ex-4: use default linker then test v-ext 0.8 algorithm with SID
    56→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    57→#
    58→#   Ex-5: use default linker then test v-ext 1.0 algorithm with SID
    59→#   ../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    60→#
    61→# Note:
    62→#   In build-system-3, the 3rd parameter is allways "riscv-sim-wrapper".
    63→#   build-system-3 will prepare a proper warpper named "riscv-sim-wrapper"
    64→#   according to the specified simulation target by users, and this wrapper will
    65→#   be used in the accuray test later.
    66→#
    67→################################################################################
    68→
    69→#######################################################
    70→# check the coverage of executed functions
    71→#######################################################
    72→function check_coverage {
    73→    local gcda_list called_fun_list fun_coverage fun_name coverage_status
    74→    # remember change directory to the folder used to keep gcov outputs
    75→    cd ${GCOV_DIR}
    76→
    77→    gcda_list=($(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.gcda))
    78→    for gcda in ${gcda_list[@]}; do
    79→        called_fun_list=$(${GCOV} -f ${gcda} | grep -w "Function" -A1)
    80→        IFS=-
    81→        called_fun_list=(${called_fun_list})
    82→        IFS=
    83→        coverage_status=""
    84→        is_coverage_fail=0
    85→
    86→        # let the function with riscv_nn prefix be shown first
    87→        for called_fun in "${called_fun_list[@]}"; do
    88→            if [[ "${called_fun}" == *"${FUN_PREFIX}"* ]]; then
    89→                fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
    90→                # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
    91→                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
    92→                    fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
    93→                    coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
    94→                    # coverage_status+=""
    95→                    if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
    96→                        is_coverage_fail=1
    97→                    fi
    98→                fi
    99→
   100→                called_fun_list=("${called_fun_list[@]/${called_fun}}")
   101→                break
   102→            fi
   103→        done
   104→
   105→        for called_fun in ${called_fun_list[@]}; do
   106→            fun_coverage=$(grep -Eo "[[:digit:]]{1,3}.[[:digit:]]{2}%" <<< ${called_fun})
   107→            # remember to ignore those functions with 0.00% coverage
   108→            if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != 0.00% ]]; then
   109→                fun_name=$(grep -w "Function" <<< ${called_fun} | cut -d "'" -f2)
   110→                coverage_status+="${fun_name},${fun_coverage},"
   111→                if [[ "${fun_coverage}" != "100.00%" ]]; then
   112→                    is_coverage_fail=1
   113→                fi
   114→            fi
   115→        done
   116→
   117→        if [ ${is_coverage_fail} -eq 1 ]; then
   118→            coverage_fail_list+=("${coverage_status}")
   119→            echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COVERAGE_FAIL_LIST}
   120→            cp $(basename ${gcda/gcda/c.gcov}) ${FAILED_GCOV_DIR}
   121→        fi
   122→
   123→        # write coverage results into file
   124→        echo ${coverage_status} >> ${COV_CSV}
   125→    done
   126→
   127→    # go back to previous path
   128→    cd ${CURRENT_PATH}
   129→}
   130→
   131→#######################################################
   132→# collect the code size of each section
   133→#   $1: test function name
   134→#######################################################
   135→calculate_code_size () {
   136→    local log_file build_cmd tmp_size_log
   137→
   138→    echo -n "${1}," >> ${SIZE_CSV}
   139→    log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}_empty.log"
   140→    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 ${EXTRA_FLAG} -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1_empty.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${EMPTY_LIB} -lm"
   141→    #use the linker specified by the user
   142→    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
   143→        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
   144→    fi
   145→
   146→    # build the executable
   147→    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
   148→
   149→    # get the size info
   150→    tmp_size_log="${LOG_DIR}/size.log"
   151→    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx       | tail -1 > ${tmp_size_log}
   152→    ${SIZE} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}_empty.adx | tail -1 >> ${tmp_size_log}
   153→
   154→    # calculate the size of each section
   155→    awk '
   156→        NR==1 {split($0,non_empty)}
   157→        NR==2 {split($0,empty)}
   158→        END {for(i=1;i<=5;i++) printf "%u,", non_empty[i] - empty[i]}
   159→    ' ${tmp_size_log} >> ${SIZE_CSV}
   160→    echo >> ${SIZE_CSV}  #new line
   161→}
   162→
   163→#######################################################
   164→# run the unit test and check the accuracy results
   165→#   $1: test function name
   166→#   $2: test function group name (for grouping excel results)
   167→#######################################################
   168→function run_and_check {
   169→    # skip the fp16 functions when the zfh extension is not enabled
   170→    if [ ${ENA_FP16} -eq 0 ] && [[ "${1}" == *"f16"* ]]; then
   171→        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
   172→        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
   173→        return
   174→    fi
   175→
   176→    # skip the integer 4-bit functions when the v-ext enabled but the VLN8 not enabled
   177→    if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ -mext-vector ]] && [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 0 ]] && [[ ${1} =~ s4 ]]; then
   178→        accuracy_unsupport_num=$((accuracy_unsupport_num + 1))
   179→        echo "$2,$1,x,x," >> ${PERF_CSV}
   180→        return
   181→    fi
   182→
   183→    log "--- $1 ---"
   184→    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
   185→    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${LINKED_LIB} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -lm"
   186→    #use the linker specified by the user
   187→    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
   188→        build_cmd="${build_cmd} -fuse-ld=${LD}"
   189→    fi
   190→
   191→    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
   192→    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
   193→    if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} == 0 ]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
   194→        # dump the disassembly
   195→        ${OBJDUMP} "${ADX_DIR}/t_${1}.adx" -S > "${OBJDUMP_DIR}/t_${1}.objdump"
   196→
   197→        log "Start at $(date)"
   198→        ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx >> ${log_file} 2>&1
   199→        ret=$?
   200→        log "End at $(date)"
   201→
   202→        # check correctness
   203→        if [ ${ret} == 0 ]; then
   204→            # check whehter the program is executed (For the case of AndeSim
   205→            # doesn't launch successfully. BTW, AndeSim doesn't return error
   206→            # code even if it enounters some issues.)
   207→            # Here we use the ALWAYS present string as the criteria whether
   208→            # the test program is executed.
   209→            grep -w 'pass\|fail' ${log_file} &> /dev/null
   210→            if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
   211→                accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
   212→                echo "Warning! There is no 'pass' or 'fail' string is parsed in this log file; therefore, the program may not executed." >> ${log_file}
   213→            else
   214→                accuracy_pass_num=$((accuracy_pass_num + 1))
   215→            fi
   216→
   217→            if [ ${ENA_GREP_PF_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
   218→                #dump inst/cycle into csv
   219→                echo -e "$2,$1,\c" >> ${PERF_CSV}
   220→                grep -w 'inst' ${log_file}  | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${PERF_CSV}
   221→                grep -w 'cycle' ${log_file} | awk '{printf "%ld,", $5}' >> ${PERF_CSV}
   222→                echo >> ${PERF_CSV}  #new line
   223→            fi
   224→
   225→            if [ ${ENA_GREP_PARAM_TO_CSV} == 1 ]; then
   226→                # following parser will work properly only as below assumptions
   227→                # are valid
   228→                #   (1) each parameter is seperated with semicolon except the
   229→                #       last one
   230→                #   (2) the parameters are surrounded by the "param_hint_begin"
   231→                #       and "param_hint_end"
   232→
   233→                #dump the parameters into csv
   234→                local param_hint_begin="___PARAMETERS_BEGIN___"
   235→                local param_hint_end="___PARAMETERS_END___"
   236→                local tmp_param_str=$(sed -n "/${param_hint_begin}/,/${param_hint_end}/p" ${log_file})
   237→
   238→                # remove all newlines
   239→                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//$'\r'/}
   240→                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//$'\n'/}
   241→                # remove hint string
   242→                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//${param_hint_begin}/}
   243→                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//${param_hint_end}/,}
   244→                # make each parameter being in one line
   245→                tmp_param_str=${tmp_param_str//;/;$'\n'}
   246→                echo -e "$2,$1,${tmp_param_str}" >> ${PARAM_CSV}
   247→            fi
   248→        else
   249→            accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
   250→        fi
   251→    else
   252→        # build the executable fail
   253→        accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")
   254→    fi
   255→
   256→    if [[ ${ENA_MEASURE_CODE_SIZE} -eq 1 ]]; then
   257→        calculate_code_size "${1}"
   258→    fi
   259→}

   261→###############################################################################
   262→# build and run the target configuration diagnosis program
   263→#  $1: source file name of the program
   264→###############################################################################
   265→function target_cfg_diagnosis {
   266→    local src_fname="target_cfg_diagnosis"
   267→    log "--- ${src_fname} ---"
   268→    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${src_fname}.log"
   269→    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -o ${ADX_DIR}/$src_fname.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/$src_fname.c ${EXTRA_FLAG}"
   270→    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
   271→
   272→    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
   273→    if [ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} == 0 ]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
   274→        log "Start at $(date)"
   275→        ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/${src_fname}.adx >> ${log_file} 2>&1
   276→        ret=$?
   277→        log "End at $(date)"
   278→    fi
   279→}

   281→#######################################################
   282→# build the executables
   283→#   $1: test function name
   284→#######################################################
   285→function build_executable {
   286→    log "--- $1 ---"
   287→    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/${1}.log"
   288→    build_cmd="${CC} ${DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS} -O3 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=${ENA_DUMP_GOLDEN} -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=${ENA_MEASURE_PERF} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_DIR}/t_$1.adx ${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_$1.c ${LINKED_LIB} ${EXTRA_FLAG} -lm"
   289→
   290→    #use the linker specified by the user
   291→    if [[ -n ${LD} ]]; then
   292→        build_cmd+=" -fuse-ld=${LD}"
   293→    fi
   294→
   295→    echo ${build_cmd} > ${log_file}
   296→    ${build_cmd} 2>>${log_file} | tee -a ${log_file}
   297→    if [[ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} != 0 ]]; then  #check the compiling successful or not
   298→        # build the executable fail
   299→        accuracy_fail_list+=("${1}")

   301→}
   302→
   303→#######################################################
   304→# Some function's algorithm implementaions are hardware
   305→# configuration dependant; thus, there are mutiple
   306→# algoirhtms for different hardware configurations and
   307→# the code coverage is impossible to be 100% as executing
   308→# the function under one hardware configuration. Here we
   309→# execute these functions with all hardware configuration
   310→# simulator warpper to make the coverage reach 100%.
   311→#######################################################
   312→function run_special_case_for_rvv () {
   313→    local sim_wrapper_list=($(ls ${SIM_WRAPPER%v*}*))
   314→    local log_file="${LOG_DIR}/special_case.log"
   315→
   316→    for sim_wrapper in "${sim_wrapper_list[@]}"; do
   317→      for fun in "${SPECIAL_CASE_RVV[@]}"; do
   318→          echo "${sim_wrapper} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${fun}.adx" >> ${log_file}
   319→          ${sim_wrapper} ${ADX_DIR}/t_${fun}.adx >> ${log_file}
   320→      done
   321→    done
   322→}

   324→#######################################################
   325→# Build the library
   326→#   $1: flags used to build the library
   327→#######################################################
   328→build_lib () {
   329→    local build_lib_stdout build_lib_stderr
   330→    build_lib_stdout=${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/build_lib.log
   331→    build_lib_stderr=${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}/build_lib_error.log
   332→
   333→    log "****************************************************"
   334→    log "Build libnn ..."
   335→    start_time
   336→    log "    LIB_CFLAGS= ${1}"
   337→    log "    Output=${build_lib_stdout}"

   339→    log
   340→
   341→    ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${1}" "${CC}" "${CURRENT_PATH}/${TEST_LOG_DIR}" > "${build_lib_stdout}" 2> "${build_lib_stderr}"
   342→    if [[ ${CC} =~ "linux" ]] && [[ ! ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ "-static" ]]; then
   343→        # build the dynamic library for glibc toolcahin ...
   344→        local all_obj_list=$(ls ${LIB_OBJ_DIR}/*.o)
   345→        local build_shared_lib_cmd="${CC} -shared -o ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/${LIB_NAME}.so ${all_obj_list}"
   346→        echo ${build_shared_lib_cmd} >> "${build_lib_stdout}"
   347→        ${build_shared_lib_cmd} >> "${build_lib_stdout}" 2> "${build_lib_stderr}"
   348→    fi

   351→    if [ ${build_lib_result} == 0 ]; then

    10→ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"

    23→    echo ""

    25→    echo ""
    26→    exit 1
    27→fi
    28→

    47→
    48→cd ${OLD_PWD}
    49→

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→#set -x
     3→
     4→OLD_PWD=`pwd`
     5→ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
     6→THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"
     7→
     8→# This file is a board simulator wrapper shell script for both nds32le-elf & nds32be-elf,
     9→# which is called during the gcc/plumhall/supertest testsuite verification flow.
    10→#
    11→# Currently, the nds32 simulator -- run binary on board -- does not run program in intuitive way.
    12→# The simulatior requires a gdb expect file in which the execution file is strictly specified.
    13→# We use this wrapper script to complete the work so that the testsuite can easily use it to do simulation.
    14→
    15→# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
    16→SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}
    17→
    18→# check whether MYPC environment variable is set up
    19→if [ -z ${MYPC} ]; then
    20→    echo
    21→    echo "Warning! MYPC environment variable (where ICEman launches on) is not set yet."
    22→    echo "GDB will take localhost as the target remote."
    23→    echo
    24→fi
    25→
    26→# check whether MYPORT environment variable is set up
    27→if [ -z ${MYPORT} ]; then
    28→    echo
    29→    echo "Error! MYPORT environment variable is not set yet."
    30→    echo "Please set MYPORT to the socket port number of ICEman for GDB connection."
    31→    echo
    32→    exit 1
    33→fi
    34→
    35→# the following variables will combine directory name and file name to form a full-path
    36→RUN_GDB=$SIMULATOR_DIR/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp
    37→
    38→# check if the execution file is available
    39→if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    40→    echo ""
    41→    echo "No such file $1"
    42→    echo ""
    43→    exit 1
    44→fi
    45→
    46→# get the executable file
    47→RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH
    48→
    49→# set gcc testsuite should be "yes" or "no", it will effect how to get return value.
    50→GCC="no"
    51→
    52→# set the board cache will be "on" or "lm". lm measns using local memory.
    53→CACHE="on"
    54→
    55→# set the board need reset-and-run or not.
    56→RESET_BOARD="on"
    57→
    58→# we should reset the board when we run first test case in every testsuite.
    59→if [ ! -f "$SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done" ]; then
    60→    RESET="on"
    61→    touch $SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done
    62→else
    63→    RESET=$RESET_BOARD
    64→fi
    65→
    66→# physically invoke simulator
    67→if test -r CVALSTDIN
    68→then
    69→	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET CVALSTDIN
    70→else
    71→	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET
    72→fi

#!/usr/bin/expect

# Set some variables

# Setup the timeout value while simulating each test case on real board.
# This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.
#   e.g. "set timeout 500"
set timeout 1200

# Setup the nds32 gdb full-path so that we can execute it to connect to aice.
# This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.
#   e.g. "set gdb /path/to/nds32le-elf-newlib-v3/bin/nds32le-elf-gdb"
set gdb riscv32-elf-gdb

# Setup the host machine that iceman is running on.
# This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.
#   e.g. "set host apc188"
set host $::env(MYPC)

#Setup the port opened by iceman connecting to real board.
# This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.
#   e.g. "set port 1234"
set port $::env(MYPORT)

send "set pagination off\n"

set elf [lindex $argv 0]
set gcc [lindex $argv 1]
set cache [lindex $argv 2]
set reset [lindex $argv 3]
set stdin [lindex $argv 4]
set gdb_prompt_pattern ".*gdb\[)] "

# Execute binary
if { $gcc == "yes" } {
  spawn $gdb -e "$elf"
} else {
  spawn $gdb -e "$elf" -return-child-result -q
}

# When timeout, will send contrlC to stop this program
proc ctrlC {} {
	send \003
	set status 123
	send "quit\n"
	expect {
		"Quit anyway?" {
			send "y\n"
		}
	}

```bash
./build.sh D25 "-funroll-loops"                   # Add extra flags
./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-linux-gcc               # Custom compiler (parameter $3)
./build.sh D25 "" riscv32-elf-clang               # Alternative compiler (clang)
```

#### B. **Compiler Selection Logic**

1. **Default compiler** (parameter $3 empty):
   ```bash
   CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
   # e.g., "riscv32-elf-gcc", "riscv64-elf-gcc"
   ```

2. **Custom compiler** (parameter $3 specified):

   ```bash
   CC="${3}"
   ```

#### C. **Compile Flags by CPU**

Different CPUs enable different extensions via `-m` flags:

| CPU | Key Flags |
|-----|-----------|
| D10, D15, D23, D25, A25, A27, A45, A46MP | `-mext-dsp` (DSP extension) |
| N225, D23 | `-mext-zc` (compressed) |
| NX27V, AX45MPV, AX46MPV, AX66 | `-mext-vector` + RVV version macros |
| nx27v/ax45mpv | `-DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000` (RVV 1.0) |
| nx25v_freya | `-DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80` (RVV 0.8) |
| 45-series | `-DENA_45_SERIES_OPT` (auto-added) |

### 2. QEMU CONFIGURATION

#### A. **QEMU Wrapper Scripts**

QEMU is invoked through **per-CPU wrapper scripts** in project directories.

**Pattern**: `/local/nick/libnn/project/<CPU>/riscv-qemu-wrapper-<variant>`

**Examples**:
- `/local/nick/libnn/project/nx25/riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx25`
- `/local/nick/libnn/project/nx27v/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_s512_b512` (v-ext variant)
- `/local/nick/libnn/project/ax46mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512`

#### B. **QEMU Wrapper Configuration Structure**

Each wrapper script:
1. **Sets QEMU binary** from environment variable:
   ```bash
   QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}     # or QEMU_SYS_RV32
   ```

2. **Specifies CPU model**:
   ```bash
   CPU=andes-nx25                                    # Simple CPU
   CPU=andes-nx27v,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=512          # Vector CPU with parameters
   ```

3. **Selects config file** (dynamic for v-ext variants):
   ```bash
   CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/ADP-AE350-NX25.cfg       # Simple config
   # For v-ext, selects from qemu_cfg/<VEC_CFG>/ directory
   CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
   ```

4. **Runs QEMU with flags**:
   ```bash
   $QEMU -readconfig $CONFIG -M andes_ae350 -cpu $CPU \
         -nographic -semihosting -bios $RUN_FILE ${EXTRA_QEMU_CONF}
   ```

#### C. **Vector Extension Configuration (VEC_CFG)**

For v-ext CPUs, vector configuration is selected via environment variable `VEC_CFG`:

```bash
# Supported values:
# zve32x, zve32f, zve64x, zve64f, zve64d (default if not set)

# Example with dynamic selection:
if [[ -z ${VEC_CFG} ]]; then
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
else
    CONFIG=${SIMULATOR_DIR}/qemu_cfg/${VEC_CFG}/ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg
fi
```

**Directory Structure**:
```
/local/nick/libnn/project/nx27v/qemu_cfg/
├── zve32f/
├── zve32x/
├── zve64d/  (default)
├── zve64f/
└── zve64x/
```

#### D. **QEMU Config Files (.cfg)**

QEMU config files specify CPU ISA features and memory configuration.

**Example** (`ADP-AE350-NX27V_dw0.cfg`):
```ini
[global]
driver = "andes-nx27v-riscv-cpu"
property = "vlen"
value = "512"

[global]
driver = "andes-nx27v-riscv-cpu"
property = "elen"
value = "64"

[andes-config "cpu"]
isa-a = "on"
isa-v = "on"
isa-vl4 = "on"
isa-vdot = "on"
```

**File Locations**: `/local/nick/libnn/project/<CPU>/qemu_cfg/<VEC_CFG>/`

#### E. **QEMU Run Mode**

Controlled in wrapper scripts:
```bash
RUN_MODE=0    # 0 = standalone mode, 1 = GDB server mode (-S -s)
```

### 3. SIMULATOR TARGETS (GDB, SID, QEMU, BOARD)

#### A. **test.sh Target Selection (Parameter $4)**

```bash
../test.sh "" <compiler> <simulator> <TARGET> <BS3/DAILY> <flags>
#                         $2          $3         $4
```

Valid targets:
- **GDB**: NDS32 GDB simulator (V3/nds32 only)
- **SID**: Andes System Integration Design simulator (for legacy hardware)
- **QEMU**: QEMU system emulator (modern, RISC-V and V-ext support)
- **BOARD**: Physical hardware via ICEman/GDB expect scripts
- **ALIOTH**: Andes-specific board variant

**Examples**:
```bash
../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3     # GDB simulator
../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3   # SID simulator
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper QEMU BS3  # QEMU simulator
../test.sh "" riscv64-linux-gcc dummy-wrapper BOARD BS3   # Physical board
```

#### B. **Wrapper Selection in test.sh**

The wrapper script is passed as parameter $3:
```bash
SIM_WRAPPER="${3}"    # Full path or command name from PATH
```

For QEMU targets, test.sh expects:
```bash
${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_DIR}/t_<test>.adx    # Executes: riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx25 <test.adx>
```

### 4. BOARD/FPGA CONFIGURATION

#### A. **Board Simulator Wrappers**

Located at: `/local/nick/libnn/project/<CPU>/riscv<N>-sim-wrapper-on-board`

**Mechanism**: Uses GDB expect scripts to connect to ICEman hardware debugger

**File Example**: `/local/nick/libnn/project/nx25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` (80 lines)

#### B. **Board Control Parameters**

```bash
MYPC=${MYPC}        # Host machine running ICEman (environment variable)
MYPORT=${MYPORT}    # Socket port for GDB-ICEman connection (required)
CACHE="on"|"lm"     # "on" for cache, "lm" for local memory
RESET_BOARD="on"    # Reset board before execution
GCC="yes"|"no"      # GCC testsuite compatibility mode
```

#### C. **GDB Expect Scripts**

Located at: `/local/nick/libnn/project/<CPU>/gdb-ice-<arch>.exp`

**Examples**:
- `/local/nick/libnn/project/d25/gdb-ice-riscv32.exp`
- `/local/nick/libnn/project/nx45/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp`

**Key Configuration** (from expect script):
```bash
set timeout 1200                    # Timeout in seconds
set gdb riscv32-elf-gdb            # GDB command
set host $::env(MYPC)              # Target hostname
set port $::env(MYPORT)            # Target port

spawn $gdb -e "$elf" -return-child-result -q
send "target remote $host:$port\n"
send "load\n"
send "c\n"
```

#### D. **Linker Scripts for Board**

Board configurations use custom linker scripts:

| CPU | Linker Script |
|-----|---------------|
| V3 (D10) | `/local/nick/libnn/project/d10/nds32.ld` |
| RV32 (D25) | `/local/nick/libnn/project/d25/ae350-xip.ld` |
| RV64 (NX27V-Qilai) | `/local/nick/libnn/project/nx27v_qilai/qilai.ld` |

Applied via test.sh when TEST_TARGET==BOARD:
```bash
if [[ ${TEST_TARGET} == "BOARD" ]] && [[ ${CC} =~ "elf" ]]; then
    if [[ -DENA_QILAI_LD ]] ; then
        DEFAULT_APP_CFLAGS="-Wl,-T ${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/project/nx27v_qilai/qilai.ld"
    fi
fi
```

**File Location**: `/local/nick/libnn/test.sh` (lines 519-530)

#### E. **Local Memory (LM) Configuration**

For FPGA/BOARD with limited memory:

```bash
ENA_LM=1    # Enable local memory mode
```

Dynamic modification via sed:
```bash
if [[ ${EXTRA_FLAG} =~ ENA_LM ]]; then
    sed -i 's/CACHE="on"/CACHE="lm"/' ${SIM_WRAPPER}
fi
```

**File Location**: `/local/nick/libnn/test.sh` (lines 1179-1208)

#### F. **SID Simulator Configuration**

SID uses `.conf` files for hardware simulation:

**Example**: `/local/nick/libnn/project/nx27v/sid.conf`

Contains:
- CPU model specification (`--conf-cpu-model nx27`)
- ISA extensions (`--conf-isa-a on`, `--conf-isa-v on`)
- Vector configuration (`--conf-VLEN 512`, `--conf-ELEN 64`)
- Cache configuration (FPGA-based):
  ```
  --conf-icache on --conf-icache-line-size 64B --conf-icache-set 128 --conf-icache-way 4
  --conf-dcache on --conf-dcache-line-size 64B --conf-dcache-set 2048 --conf-dcache-way 4
  ```
- Memory delay: `--conf-memory-delay 30`

```
    ↓

    ↓
[Override compiler] → "custom-cc" parameter
    ↓
make -f Makefile_lib.mak CROSS_COMPILE="riscv32-elf-" CC="custom-cc" CFLAGS="..."
    ↓
[Build libnn.a]
```

### 6. TEST EXECUTION FLOW

```
test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx25 QEMU BS3
    ↓
[Compiler → CC] → riscv64-elf-gcc
[Wrapper → SIM_WRAPPER] → riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx25 (from PATH)
[Target → TEST_TARGET] → QEMU
    ↓
[For QEMU]:
    - Call riscv-qemu-wrapper-nx25 <test.adx>
    - Wrapper sources: QEMU=${QEMU_SYS_RV64}
    - Wrapper loads: ADP-AE350-NX25.cfg
    - QEMU spawns with: -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx25
    ↓
[For BOARD]:
    - Call riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board <test.adx>
    - Wrapper expects: MYPC (host) and MYPORT (port) env vars
    - Wrapper spawns: expect gdb-ice-riscv64.exp
    - GDB connects: target remote $MYPC:$MYPORT
    ↓
[Run test and collect results]
```

### 7. KEY ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES

| Variable | Purpose | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `QEMU_SYS_RV64` | Path to QEMU RV64 binary | `/opt/qemu/bin/qemu-system-riscv64` |
| `QEMU_SYS_RV32` | Path to QEMU RV32 binary | `/opt/qemu/bin/qemu-system-riscv32` |
| `MYPC` | Physical board host (for BOARD target) | `192.168.1.10` or `localhost` |
| `MYPORT` | ICEman port on MYPC (for BOARD target) | `1234` |
| `VEC_CFG` | Vector extension config (for v-ext QEMU) | `zve64d` (default), `zve32x`, `zve64f`, etc. |
| `PATH` | Must include toolchain and simulator locations | Export in shell startup |
